title: JavaScript Syntax Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential rules for writing clean, error-free JavaScript code.

questions.question: JavaScript is 'Case-Sensitive,' which means that 'myVariable' and 'myvariable' are ________?
Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript?
options: Using camelCase for naming variables and functions.

questions.question: Which symbol is used at the end of a JavaScript statement (e.g., 'let x = 5;') to tell the browser it's a single command?
Which JavaScript keyword is used specifically for defining a 'Constant' value (a value that will NEVER change)?
What happens when you write a comment (//) in your JavaScript file?
options: The browser ignores the entire line, allowing you to explain your code.
It makes the site load much slower.
